canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, CASIE is consistently characterized as a dataset for event extraction and event detection within the cybersecurity domain. The papers agree on its core purpose, which is to identify security-related incidents from sources described as technical reports, cyber threat reports, and news. Several sources specify that CASIE defines five event types: Databreach, Phishing, Ransom, Discover, and Patch. One common definition further notes that the dataset includes "trigger and argument annotations." In practice, CASIE is used in experimental evaluations, sometimes alongside other information extraction benchmarks, to assess model performance. For instance, it is explicitly used as one of several datasets to measure the `Generalization` of a proposed method.
papers: LILaC: Late Interacting in Layered Component Graph for Open-domain Multimodal Multihop Retrieval
Leveraging Knowledge Graph-EnhancedLLMs for Context-Aware Medical Consultation
Cross-Lingual and Cross-Cultural Variation in Image Descriptions
FactCG: Enhancing Fact Checkers with Graph-Based Multi-Hop Data

The papers agree on its core purpose, which is to identify security-related incidents from sources described as technical reports, cyber threat reports, and news. Several sources specify that CASIE defines five event types: Databreach, Phishing, Ransom, Discover, and Patch. One common definition further notes that the dataset includes "trigger and argument annotations."
